info.title: Iopole EDI
info.description:
## Iopole API

To use the Iopole APIs, you must have a **`clientId`** and a **`clientSecret`**.

You can obtain these credentials by one of the following methods:
- 📧 Contact the Iopole team at **support@iopole.com**
- 🧪 Connect to **[Iopole Labs](https://labs.iopole.io)** to create your own sandbox environment

### 📚 API Documentation
- 🧪 **Preproduction**: [https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/](https://docs.ppd.iopole.fr/)
- 🚀 **Production**: [https://docs.iopole.com/](https://docs.iopole.com/)

### 🛠️ Support & Monitoring (production only)
- 📈 **System status**: [https://uptime.iopole.com](https://uptime.iopole.com)
  - View real-time platform availability
  - Subscribe to incident notifications
  - Receive alerts for planned maintenance periods
- 📝 **Release notes**: [https://release.iopole.com](https://release.iopole.com)

get.description: Download EDI message original file for the given message identifier.

parameters.name: messageId
parameters.description: The technical ID you received and representing the message.

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

403.description: Forbidden - The server understood the request, but it refuses to authorize it.

get.summary: Download archive file containing all message related files
get.description: Download an archive file (zip) containing all message related files.

parameters.name: messageId
parameters.description: The technical ID you received and representing the message.

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

403.description: Forbidden - The server understood the request, but it refuses to authorize it.

201.description: Returns created Iopole EDI message identifier.

type.description: The type of the response object.

id.description: The unique identifier for the created element.

schema.description: Response model for the element creation response.

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication is required and has failed or has not yet been provided.

403.description: Forbidden - The server understood the request, but it refuses to authorize it.
